sections.title: A: Word formation
sections.instructions: Use the word given in capitals at the end of each line to form a word that fits in the gap in the same line.

items.sentence: My aunt began her career as a shop ...... before becoming the manager. (ASSIST)
The new business became very ...... after only two years. (SUCCESS)
The quick decision ...... the project from failure. (SAVE)
Our team leader gives advice, but she is never ...... . (BOSS)
All the ...... received extra training last month. (EMPLOY)
Teaching is a rewarding but demanding ...... . (OCCUPY)
The company made an ...... announcement about the new offices. (OFFICE)
After thirty-five years at the school, Mr Harris finally reached ...... . (RETIRE)
The designer became ...... after creating a very popular app. (FAME)
Now that she's ......, she spends more time travelling. (RETIRE)
Lisa is responsible ......
options: A on your qualifications and experience.
B about the change in the timetable.
C to the old manager as 'impossible to please'.
E for a position at the new hotel.
F for training the new members of staff.
G as a photographer or a graphic designer.
H with his job and wants a new challenge.

sections.title: C: Key Word Transformation
sections.instructions: Complete each second sentence using the word given, so that it has a similar meaning to the first sentence. Write between two and five words.

items.sentence:
The room is so noisy that I can't study. **too**
The room is ...... me to study.

I can't go to bed early because I have to finish this report. **up**
I have to ...... late to finish this report.

He is too inexperienced to lead the team. **enough**
He isn't ...... to lead the team.

The director cancelled the interview. **off**
The director ...... the interview.
The instructions were ...... confusing that nobody understood them.
It was ...... a busy morning that we forgot to have a break.
She isn't confident ...... to give the presentation alone.
The meeting room is ...... small for twenty people.
My brother works ...... than I do.
This is ...... difficult exercise in the test.
The new computer is much ...... than the old one.
She has ...... experience than the other candidates.
He was ...... nervous to speak clearly.
It was ...... useful advice that I wrote it down.
Your pronunciation is getting ...... and .......
The course isn't practical ...... for beginners.
It was ...... a long interview that I felt exhausted afterwards.
The second applicant was ...... than the first one.

sections.title: E: Complete the sentences
sections.instructions: Write one word in each gap.

items.sentence: He applied ...... a job at the local museum.
After two years, she finally got a ...... and became team leader.
